description: The insurer's evolving estimate of the ultimate cost of a claim (or one exposure of it), set early and adjusted over the claim's life. Notarize-only audit record — premature reserve takedown or inflation is a known abuse, so each reserve change is recorded with its amount, type, and basis, attributed and tamper-evident. Reserve history is a chain of these records linked by supersedesRef; the latest non-superseded reserve is the current one. AGLedger records the reserve and its stated basis; it does not judge whether the reserve is actuarially correct.

exposureId.description: Optional. Scope the reserve to one exposure of a multi-exposure claim.

reserveType.description: indemnity = expected payout to/for the claimant; expense = loss-adjustment expense; combined = both.

basis.description: Why the reserve is set at this amount (the audit-relevant justification).

supersedesRef.description: Optional. recordId of the prior reserve record this change supersedes, forming the reserve history chain.
